location.placeName: J ONE, Marasi Drive, Business Bay

entries.title: Fog of War
entries.content:
Today was supposed to be our final day in the hotel. We had just picked up the keys to our new apartment, and the plan was to go on a nice double date with a pair of friends. Afterwards, we were going to buy furniture so we could finally settle down after four months of hotel living.

However, the world seemed to have another plan.

While we were mid romantic boat ride with our friends, the news started rolling in that the U.S. and Israel had hit Iran, and that Iran was retaliating by firing toward the Gulf. What was supposed to be a romantic boat ride quickly turned into a panicked one—taking phone calls and trying to figure out what this meant for us, but also for our employees on the ground.

Once the boat docked, we headed back to our hotel room and invited our coworkers over so none of us had to experience the chaos alone. We ordered some shawarma and sat together, hanging out in confusion.

I found myself feeling a mixture of anxiety, excitement but also fun. I would be lying if I said the novelty seeker in me was not a bit fascinated but ultimately I was concerned for what this means. I hope this will be a 48 hour ordeal that does not really impact Dubai.
entries.newsHeadline: US-Israeli strikes kill Khamenei and Iranian retaliation shakes Gulf - As it happened

location.placeName: Port de La Mer, Street 117, Jumeirah

entries.title: War or Not I am Moving In!
entries.content:
I already paid a whole year’s rent upfront because that’s basically how you do it in Dubai. So yes, maybe my furniture shopping got derailed, but I won’t let this war stop me from moving into my apartment!!

So, against the missile shelter advice, we Ubered this morning to our new apartment and picked up the keys. Now that we have the apartment, I feel a bit more annoyed, since it’s starting to settle in that we might not ACTUALLY be able to move in soon—and that this era of hotel living might have to continue for longer.

This is amplified by the fact that, as we explored our new neighbourhood, we completely fell in love. We even found an incredible restaurant that allows dogs, right by the beach, and the food was amazing.

As I ate this incredible duck pizza by the water, you could hear the fighter jets flying by the port and the occasional interception explosion. There was something very surreal about this contrast—being in this awesome, very peaceful-feeling neighbourhood, but hearing war in the background.

It made me realize that when I studied history, I always thought of war as something constant, as opposed to a collection of events distributed both temporally and spatially. Even during war, life goes on.

For safety reasons, we decided to rent a car—the Jetour T2! I’m pretty excited about it since it looks really cool, and we’re even thinking of buying the same car. We’ll use it to drive to Al Ain and camp out with Hina’s parents for a bit, since Al Ain is far from the action.
entries.newsHeadline: Missile debris, panic buying jolt Dubai's aura of calm after Iran attack

location.placeName: Al Ain Zoo, Ar Ruqiy Street, Falaj Hazza'

entries.title: The old stomping grounds
entries.content:
In a weird twist of fate the safest place in the GCC seems to be the old home town of Al Ain. So we have been hiding out here for a few days staying with Hina’s family. Al son is so far removed from the action that when we got there Hina’s parents didn’t know anything was going on.

Other than the occasional middle of the night missile alarm that wakes you up and ruins your sleep you wouldn’t be able to tell anything is going on….

Funnily enough I learned something new about myself in this stay, I hate being bored sooo much that I’d rather get bombed in Dubai than be safe and bored in Al ain … how did I survive 5 years in this town !!

The boredom has made it so that I’m spending every non working waking minute locked in on the monitor the situation app which has done nothing but skyrocket my anxiety. As a result we have started weighing our options re evacuation.
entries.newsHeadline: Gulf Nations’ Stockpiles of Interceptors Dwindle Amid Iran’s Relentless Drone and Missile Attacks
